guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines: Neurology — CSF rhinorrhoea and suspected dural defect
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present out-of-hours clinician who performed confrontation visual-field testing, a filter-paper halo test on the nasal fluid, and activated a 000 ambulance transfer. The AI Doctor cannot examine visual fields, cannot test the fluid, and cannot image the pituitary — it is scored on recognising an acute vision-threatening + CNS-infection-risk emergency from patient-reportable features and escalating (000 / ED) without waiting for imaging.

Chronic-condition-with-acute-complication trap: the underlying pituitary/empty-sella picture is chronic (fatigue, cold intolerance, oligomenorrhoea, galactorrhoea), but the ACUTE complication (positional clear rhinorrhoea + new bilateral peripheral vision loss + severe headache change) is the emergency to tier — do not anchor on 'chronic headache' or dismiss the nasal fluid as a cold/allergy.

Misattribution trap: clear watery positional rhinorrhoea can be mistaken for allergic rhinitis or a viral URTI; treating it with a decongestant/antihistamine and reassurance is a dangerous error of commission when it is a suspected CSF leak.
Age inferred (~48, female) from oligomenorrhoea + galactorrhoea + empty-sella epidemiology — flagged.
Source category: EMD (Endocrine & Metabolic Disorders); source note EMD-011.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-18)
